steps.title: Use the data directly
steps.content: This is a linear table. As $x$ increases by $1$, $y$ decreases by $3$, so the slope is $m=-3$. Use the point $(-8,12)$ in $f(x)=-3x+b$: $$12=-3(-8)+b$$ $$12=24+b$$ $$b=-12$$ Therefore $$f(x)=-3x-12$$

steps.title: Find the x-intercept
steps.content: The $x$-intercept is where $f(x)=0$. Set $-3x-12=0$, which gives $-3x=12$ and $x=-4$. So the intercept is $(-4,0)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is $(-4,0)$, so B is correct.
